source_docs.title: Characterization of directed differentiation by high-throughput single-cell RNA-Seq

oligos.name: E3V6NEXT (barcoded RT primer)
oligos.role: Reverse-transcription primer carrying the well-specific cell barcode, UMI and poly(dT) capture region; anneals to the mRNA poly(A) tail and primes first-strand cDNA synthesis.
oligos.notes: 5' biotin (/5Biosg/). [BC6] = 6 bp well/cell barcode from Supplementary Table S2 (384-plex; barcode set 1 for D1/D2, set 2 for D3). N10 = 10 bp UMI. T30 = 30-nt poly(dT). VN = anchor (V = A/C/G, N = any). Ordered from IDT; 1 pmol per well.

oligos.name: E5V6NEXT (universal template-switching oligo)
oligos.role: Template-switching oligo; its terminal riboguanosines base-pair with the untemplated Cs added by the RT at the 3' end of the first-strand cDNA, appending the universal 5' adapter.
oligos.notes: Written 5'->3' as iCiGiC-ACACTCTTTCCCTACACGACGC-rGrGrG. iC = iso-dC, iG = iso-dG (non-natural bases reducing background), rG = riboguanosine. Ordered from Eurogentec; 1 pmol per well.

oligos.name: E3V6NEXT (bulk, 16 bp UMI version)
oligos.role: RT primer used for bulk SCRB-seq DGE libraries; identical to E3V6NEXT but omits the well-specific 6 bp barcode, using a 16 bp UMI instead.
oligos.notes: 5' biotin (/5Biosg/). N16 = 16 bp UMI (no cell barcode). Used at 10 pmol for bulk 10 ng total-RNA libraries. From IDT.

oligos.name: SINGV6 (single-primer PCR primer)
oligos.role: Single primer used for full-length cDNA pre-amplification after pooling; anneals to the universal ACACTCTTTCCCTACACGACGC adapter present on both ends of the template-switched cDNA.
oligos.notes: 5' biotin (/5Biosg/). Used with Advantage 2 Polymerase Mix; 10 pmol. From IDT.

oligos.name: P5NEXTPT5 (custom i5/P5 library primer)
oligos.role: Custom P5 library-amplification primer that replaces the Nextera XT i5 primer, grafting the P5 flow-cell sequence onto the barcode/UMI end and serving as the Read 1 priming site.
oligos.notes: 3'-terminal * = phosphorothioate bonds (last 4 linkages: ...CCG*A*T*C*T*). Used at 5 uM in the modified Nextera library amplification. From IDT. Doubles as the custom Read 1 sequencing-primer region.

oligos.name: Nextera XT i7 index primer (N7xx)
oligos.role: Standard Nextera XT i7 index/library-amplification primer that grafts the P7 flow-cell sequence and the i7 sample index onto the transposon (s7) end during library amplification.

components.name: i7 index
components.role: 8 bp Nextera sample index (decoded by the index read)

oligos.notes: Supplied by the Nextera XT kit (i5 replaced by P5NEXTPT5, i7 retained). Constant regions confirmed from the final-library diagram (top strand ...CTGTCTCTTATACACATCTCCGAGCCCACGAGAC[i7]ATCTCGTATGCCGTCTTCTGCTTG).

oligos.name: Nextera transposon end (s7 / mosaic end)
oligos.role: Transposon adapter sequence deposited on the fragmented 3' cDNA end by the Nextera Tn5 transposase during tagmentation; provides the Read 2 / i7-side priming region.
oligos.notes: Read from the tagmented-product diagram (bottom strand 3'-...GACAGAGAATATGTGTAGAGGCTCGGGTGCTCTG-5'). Contains the mosaic-end reverse complement (CTGTCTCTTATACACATCT) fused to the s7 sequence.

oligos.name: Custom Read 1 sequencing primer
oligos.role: Primes Read 1, reading the cell barcode + UMI (+ start of poly-dT).
oligos.notes: Corresponds to the grafted P5NEXTPT5 region (see p5nextpt5). Reads 17 cycles into [BC6][UMI].

oligos.name: Nextera i7 index sequencing primer
oligos.role: Primes the 8-cycle index read that decodes the i7 Nextera sample barcode.
oligos.notes: Standard Nextera Index 1 (i7) read primer; annealing site present on the s7/transposon end of the library.

oligos.name: Nextera Read 2 sequencing primer
oligos.role: Primes the 34-cycle second read that sequences the 3' end of the cDNA fragment.
oligos.notes: Standard Nextera Read 2 primer; complementary to the CTGTCTCTTATACACATCTCCGAGCCCACGAGAC transposon region, reads leftward into the cDNA insert.

final_library.annotated_library_sequence: AATGATACGGCGACCACCGAGATCTAC | ACACTCTTTCCCTACACGACGCTCTTCCGATCT | [CELL_BARCODE:6] | [UMI:10] | TTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTT VN | [CDNA] | CTGTCTCTTATACACATCTCCGAGCCCACGAGAC | [SAMPLE_INDEX:8] | ATCTCGTATGCCGTCTTCTGCTTG
annotation_lines: ACACTCTTTCCCTACACGACGCTCTTCCGATCT  -- universal adapter / Read 1 priming region (TruSeq-R1-like)
[CELL_BARCODE:6]  -- well-specific cell barcode (6 bp, Supplementary Table S2)
TTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTT VN  -- 30-nt poly(dT) capture + VN anchor (former mRNA poly(A))
CTGTCTCTTATACACATCTCCGAGCCCACGAGAC  -- Nextera transposon / s7 end (Read 2 priming region)

library_generation.title: Template-switching reverse transcription
library_generation.summary: Single-cell mRNA is reverse-transcribed with the barcoded poly(dT) RT primer E3V6NEXT, tagging each cDNA with a well barcode and UMI.
library_generation.note: Cells are FACS-sorted into 384-well plates; ERCC spike-in is added and the template-switching RT reaction is run with SmartScribe (D1/D2) or Maxima H Minus (D3). E3V6NEXT anneals to the mRNA poly(A) tail (T30VN) and copies the transcript; the RT adds a few untemplated C's at the cDNA 3' end.

library_generation.title: Template switching (E5V6NEXT)
library_generation.summary: The universal TSO E5V6NEXT base-pairs with the cDNA's terminal C's and the RT extends across it, appending the universal 5' adapter.
library_generation.note: E5V6NEXT terminal rGrGrG pairs with the CCC overhang; the iso-dC/iso-dG bases suppress background. This yields cDNA flanked by universal adapters on both ends.

library_generation.title: Full-length barcoded cDNA
library_generation.summary: Second-strand synthesis completes a double-stranded, barcoded, adapter-flanked full-length cDNA.
library_generation.note: Both ends now carry the ACACTCTTTCCCTACACGACGC universal sequence, enabling single-primer PCR.

library_generation.title: Pool, ExoI, single-primer PCR (SINGV6)
library_generation.summary: cDNA from all 384 wells is pooled, purified, Exonuclease I-treated, and amplified by single-primer PCR with SINGV6.
library_generation.note: Because both ends share the universal adapter, one primer (SINGV6, ACACTCTTTCCCTACACGACGC) amplifies the whole pool with Advantage 2 polymerase; products are AMPure-purified and quantified.

library_generation.title: Tagmentation (Nextera XT)
library_generation.summary: Full-length cDNA is fragmented and tagged with Nextera transposon ends by Tn5, enriching for 3' ends.
library_generation.note: Tn5 inserts the transposon (s7/mosaic-end) sequence at random positions; fragments retaining the barcode/UMI end are carried forward.

library_generation.title: Modified library amplification (P5NEXTPT5 + i7)
library_generation.summary: The tagmented pool is amplified with the custom P5NEXTPT5 (i5 replacement) and a standard Nextera i7 primer, adding P5, P7 and the i7 sample index.
library_generation.note: Replacing the Nextera i5 primer with P5NEXTPT5 grafts the P5 sequence next to the barcode/UMI so Read 1 reads the cell barcode + UMI; the i7 primer adds P7 and the sample index. Library is size-selected to 300-800 bp.

library_sequencing.note: 17 cycles read the 6 bp well/cell barcode + 10 bp UMI (16 informative nt, +1). Primer is identical to the top strand, so it anneals to the bottom strand and is extended 5'->3' rightward, reproducing the top-strand [BC6][UMI]. Filtering: first 6 bases Q>=10 matching a designed barcode, next 10 (UMI) Q>=30.
library_sequencing.diagram:
Read 1 (BC6 + UMI): primer = P5NEXTPT5, anneals to bottom strand, reads 5'->3' -->
5'-AATGATACGGCGACCACCGAGATCTACACTCTTTCCCTACACGACGCTCTTCCGATCT---------------->
5'-AATGATACGGCGACCACCGAGATCTACACTCTTTCCCTACACGACGCTCTTCCGATCT[BC6][UMI](T)30VN-[cDNA]...-3'  (top)
3'-TTACTATGCCGCTGGTGGCTCTAGATGTGAGAAAGGGATGTGCTGCGAGAAGGCTAGA[BC6][UMI](A)30BN-[cDNA]...-5'  (bottom = template)

library_sequencing.note: 8-cycle index read decodes the 8 bp i7 Nextera sample barcode. The primer is identical to the top-strand transposon sequence lying just 5' of [i7], so it anneals to the bottom strand and reads 5'->3' rightward into the index, reproducing the top-strand i7 sequence.
library_sequencing.diagram:
i7 index read (Nextera sample index): primer anneals to bottom strand, reads 5'->3' -->
    CTGTCTCTTATACACATCTCCGAGCCCACGAGAC---------->
...-CTGTCTCTTATACACATCTCCGAGCCCACGAGAC[i7]ATCTCGTATGCCGTCTTCTGCTTG-3'  (top)
...-GACAGAGAATATGTGTAGAGGCTCGGGTGCTCTG[i7]TAGAGCATACGGCAGAAGACGAAC-5'  (bottom = template)

library_sequencing.note: 34-cycle read sequences the 3' end of the cDNA fragment (assigned to RefSeq genes). The primer equals the bottom-strand sequence over the transposon end, so it anneals to the top strand and is extended 5'->3' leftward into the cDNA insert.
library_sequencing.diagram:
Read 2 (3' cDNA): primer = Nextera R2, anneals to top strand, reads 5'->3' <--
5'-...VN-[cDNA]-CTGTCTCTTATACACATCTCCGAGCCCACGAGAC[i7]...-3'  (top = template)
3'-...BN-[cDNA]-GACAGAGAATATGTGTAGAGGCTCGGGTGCTCTG[i7]...-5'  (bottom)
   <------------GACAGAGAATATGTGTAGAGGCTCGGGTGCTCTG-5'  (R2 primer, 3' end at left, reads leftward)

title: SCRB-seq
description: SCRB-seq (Single Cell RNA Barcoding and Sequencing) is a 3' digital gene-expression single-cell RNA-seq protocol. Single cells are FACS-sorted into 384-well plates and reverse-transcribed with a template-switching RT using a well-specific 6 bp barcode + 10 bp UMI on a poly(dT) RT primer (E3V6NEXT) and a universal template-switching oligo (E5V6NEXT). Barcoded cDNA from all 384 wells is pooled, single-primer PCR amplified, and fragmented/tagmented with Nextera XT (transposase) to enrich for 3' ends and preserve strand information. Read 1 decodes the cell barcode + UMI, an i7 index read decodes the Nextera sample index, and Read 2 sequences the 3' cDNA fragment.

reference.label: Characterization of directed differentiation by high-throughput single-cell RNA-Seq

original_publication.title: Characterization of directed differentiation by high-throughput single-cell RNA-Seq

authors.name: Magali Soumillon

authors.name: Alexander van Oudenaarden
authors.affiliation: Hubrecht Institute, Royal Netherlands Academy of Arts and Sciences and University Medical Center Utrecht, The Netherlands

authors.name: Tarjei S. Mikkelsen
authors.affiliation: Broad Institute, Cambridge, MA; Harvard Stem Cell Institute and Department of Stem Cell and Regenerative Biology, Harvard University, Cambridge, MA

throughput.summary: 12,832 single-cell DGE profiles (76% of wells) from 44 x 384-well plates; ~165,000 reads/well; mean 2,602 (initial) and 3,336 (optimized) unique mRNAs per cell.
throughput.cells: 12,832 cells profiled (out of ~16,896 sorted wells; day 0-14 hASC differentiation time courses)
throughput.rna: 1,000-~10,000 unique mRNAs (UMIs) captured per cell; ~1-2% mRNA detection efficiency relative to smFISH

publication.statistical_model: Digital gene expression quantified as unique UMI counts per gene per well; downstream analysis by principal component analysis (PCA) on scaled UMI-normalized expression, pairwise Pearson correlation, and gene-set enrichment (GSEA), with Benjamini-Hochberg FDR control. No parametric count distribution (e.g., Poisson/negative-binomial) is fitted to the UMI counts.

other.label: Library complexity
other.value: ~4-fold higher mean complexity than a prior high-throughput protocol (Jaitin et al. 2014)

other.label: Efficiency
other.value: ~2x fewer enzymatic reactions, purifications and liquid transfers than the previous high-throughput protocol

data_processing.summary: Reads are demultiplexed by the well-specific cell barcode, aligned to RefSeq transcripts, and collapsed by UMI to yield per-cell digital gene expression (unique UMI counts per gene, 'survey-depth DGE profiles'); cell-to-cell variation is then dissected by PCA on UMI-normalized expression together with pairwise correlation and gene-set enrichment analysis (GSEA) under Benjamini-Hochberg FDR control.
